prompt: Implement a FieldOps Service & Environmental Analytics Suite for offline service stores to run end-to-end scheduling, fulfillment, and performance oversight from a single English-language web station. The Layui-based UI provides a role-gated sign-in and workspace selector that binds each user to a specific store and workstation for shift accountability, with roles defined as Customer, Front Desk, Technician, Store Manager, Finance, and Administrator. Customers place service orders at the front desk or via a kiosk-style view, applying locally issued coupons and entering optional invoice details; the interface must show real-time validation feedback, a clear "amount due" breakdown in USD, and an on-screen receipt after confirmation. Front Desk can create, edit, and cancel orders with a required cancellation reason, assign technicians, and record completion timestamps; Technicians can accept assigned jobs, log work notes, and mark completion but cannot alter pricing. Store Managers see operational dashboards for transaction volume, average fulfillment time, cancellation rate, and complaint rate with date ranges in MM/DD/YYYY and export to CSV for offline sharing; Finance can review daily cash drawer totals and reconciliation screens. The Operations and Analytics dashboard additionally tracks activity, conversion, retention, content quality for internal announcements, and zero-result search rate for the order/catalog search, while allowing admins to define events and run A/B configurations that switch UI copy or coupon presentation for a fixed window (e.g., 14 days) with a built-in holdout group. The backend uses ThinkPHP to implement a REST-style API consumed by the Layui frontend over the local network, enforcing tiered permissions on every endpoint and writing immutable, auditable operation logs that capture user, role, store/workstation, timestamp, action, and before/after values; logs must be searchable and retained for at least 7 years. MySQL persists users, roles, store/workstation bindings, orders, refunds, coupons, announcements, events, experiments, and metrics. Authentication is strictly offline with username + password, minimum 12 characters with complexity rules and lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes; passwords are salted and hashed, and sensitive fields (e.g., taxpayer/invoice identifiers) are encrypted at rest with key material stored on the server. Payments are handled as offline tenders (cash, card-present recorded, or house account) without any gateways; the system still generates payment records, refund orders, and end-of-day reconciliation statements, and flags discrepancies when the expected total differs from counted tender by more than $1.00. Environmental metric fusion and derived feature computation ingests local sensor feeds or CSV imports, aligns records by time (default 1-minute buckets) and optional zone mapping within a store, assigns confidence labels based on completeness, computes moving averages and rate-of-change, and produces a comfort index with configurable thresholds; every derived value must store computation lineage back to raw inputs and versioned formulas. Data cleansing & standardization runs during import for customer-entered or partner-provided datasets (e.g., job title, company, city, salary, education/experience requirements) using deterministic parsing, denoising, deduplication, and entity alignment with company normalization and similar-role merging, producing traceable cleansing logs and allowing authorized admins to approve or roll back a batch before it affects reporting.
